content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.6.1 / 5.6.3 WARN — RatingGateView.swift gates users by star selection: stars≥4 routed to a direct `itms-apps://…action=write-review` link while lower ratings are diverted to a private feedback form, and description.txt explicitly asks users to "rate us 5 stars" (App/RatingGateView.swift:11-19; fastlane/metadata/en-US/description.txt:1).

Pierre: This is a textbook rating-manipulation dark pattern — filtering unhappy users away from the public App Store review and only funneling happy ones there, combined with a custom pre-rating UI, is exactly what 5.6.1/5.6.3 prohibit. Apple wants all users to have equal access to rating via the native `SKStoreReviewController.requestReview` / `RequestReviewAction` API, not a custom gate tied to app-internal star selection. Replace this flow with the system review prompt shown unconditionally (no gating logic, no manual write-review URL) and remove the "rate us 5 stars" copy from the description.
